persons.arguments_against: The dossier flags the single-initial/namesake trap: the bio is just 'George' (initial G, no middle), there are 2 Lamond officials and 2 Lamond biographies, so a same-name relative could in principle be the staff-list 'G. Lamond', and there is no honour to cross-check (honour FAIL). This fails because the single-initial caution explicitly permits merging when there is a specific position match OR multi-edition co-occurrence -- here both are present and exceptional. The stint's office is a singular, distinctive compound post, 'Registrar of Deeds, and Registrar-General of Births, Deaths, and Marriages', which matches the bio's terminal appointment verbatim ('registrar of deeds and distributor of stamps, and registrar-general of births, deaths, and marriages, 1878', position similarity 100), and the bio's 1878 appointment flows directly into the stint's 1879-1900 tenure with no gap. Five edition-years co-occur at similarity 100. Only one person holds this singular registrarship at a time, so a second G. Lamond cannot account for it.

evidence_cited.fact: registrar of deeds and distributor of stamps, and registrar-general of births, deaths, and marriages, 1878

persons.would_overturn: Evidence of a second G. Lamond who held the Natal Registrar of Deeds / Registrar-General post would reverse this.

notes: Single-initial bio cleared by an exact singular-office match plus 5-year edition co-occurrence at similarity 100, and continuity from the bio's 1878 appointment. High-confidence match.
